title: paraderris piscatoria
intro: Paraderris piscatoria, also known as the Fish Poison Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. It is a small tree or shrub that contains toxic compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The extracts from Paraderris piscatoria have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, or ointments, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits.
